Caption_EN: October in China is *chef's kiss*. Clear skies. Mild temperatures. Autumn colours everywhere. No oppressive summer heat. No winter chill. This tour keeps it simple: iconic sites, clear itinerary, expert guides who bring the stories to life. Caption_EN: Two ancient cities. Endless history. One unforgettable journey. Beijing is imperial China: the Great Wall snaking through mountains, the Forbidden City's grandeur, hutongs where daily life unfolds as it has for centuries. Xi'an is Tang Dynasty: the Terracotta Warriors standing eternal guard, Tang City Walls where emperors once ruled, temples and gardens steeped in Buddhist tradition. A Tale of Two Cities connects them via high-speed rail in 4 comfortable hours — and brings them to life through expert guides, carefully chosen hotels, and an itinerary that's clear and unhurried. ✓ 10 days ✓ October ready from Auckland ✓ All flights, land transport, 4-star hotels, guides, and most meals included ✓ Many NZ passport holders may qualify for visa-free entry — confirm for your itinerary before booking. Caption_EN: 'Do I need a visa to visit China?' The answer isn't always 'yes' — and that's good news. Many New Zealand passport holders can enter China for leisure travel **without a visa** under the country's temporary visa-free policy. But — and this is important — the rules depend on: ✓ Your specific travel dates ✓ Your planned itinerary ✓ How long you're staying ✓ Your reason for travel. We get this question constantly. And we always say the same thing: **confirm before you book**. Caption_EN: You don't need much to have a great China trip. But the right stuff makes all the difference. October packing checklist: ✓ **Light layers** — mornings cool, afternoons warm; sweater + t-shirt combo ✓ **Comfortable walking shoes** — seriously, you'll do 15,000+ steps daily ✓ **Light rain jacket** — autumn showers happen; waterproof matters ✓ **Sunscreen + sunglasses** — UV is stronger at higher altitude ✓ **Hat or cap** — sun protection + convenient ✓ **Small scarf** — versatile, warm, and honestly just adds to the vibe ✓ **Day backpack** — 20L, good shoulder support ✓ **Modest clothing** — temples + cultural sites; shoulders/knees covered ✓ **Phone charger + adaptor** — China uses different outlets; bring a universal adaptor. Pro tip: Many hotels in China offer laundry service, so you don't need to pack for 10 days of outfits. Many NZ passport holders may qualify for visa-free entry — confirm for your itinerary before booking.

Caption_EN: Why do poets write about West Lake? Stand at its edge on a misty morning, and you'll understand. Classical Jiangnan — the region of water, gardens, and ancient towns just west of Shanghai — has captivated Chinese artists for centuries. There's a reason: the light, the stillness, the sense of time moving differently here. Shanghai & Surroundings brings you to this world. In Suzhou, you'll walk through gardens that took centuries to perfect — every stone, every plant, every reflection carefully considered. In Hangzhou, you'll rest by West Lake and understand why emperors built their retreats here. You'll taste regional cuisine that's lighter and more delicate than northern China. You'll move at a pace that lets the experience sink in. ✓ Suzhou: Garden masterpieces, silk markets, water-town charm ✓ Wuxi: Sunrise on the Grand Canal ✓ Xinshi: Peaceful lakeside towns ✓ Hangzhou: West Lake, Dragon Well tea, misty mornings ✓ Shanghai: Modern energy and historic Bund. 10 days. Water-town elegance. October ready. Many NZ passport holders may qualify for visa-free entry — confirm for your itinerary before booking.
